news.headline: Amazon Teamsters launch historic strike at hundreds of facilities, demanding union recognition and fair treatment. 6 a.m. start, largest in American history.
news.summary: Today at 6 a.m., workers nationwide initiated what is now the biggest strike against Amazon in U.S. history, demanding union recognition and fair treatment. Teamsters from various regions have joined forces in this movement, reflecting a growing momentum as more employees advocate for their rights.

news.headline: Primis Financial Corp. reauthorizes stock repurchase program for up to 740,600 shares, compliant with Nasdaq rules.
news.summary: Primis Financial Corp. (NASDAQ: FRST) headquartered in McLean, Virginia, revealed today that its board of directors has sanctioned a stock repurchase initiative for acquiring a maximum of 740,600 of the Company's common shares.

news.headline: APCU/Center Parc Credit Union partners with MarkIII to boost credit access for members, streamlining loan approvals.
news.summary: In Atlanta on December 19, 2024, APCU/Center Parc Credit Union (APCU/Center Parc) revealed a new partnership with MarkIII (MKIII) to boost credit opportunities for its members. This agreement aims to expedite loan approval processes, increasing the number of approved loans for members.

news.headline: $6.6T in options set to expire this Friday, causing traders to prepare for heightened volatility.
neffy® (Epinephrine Nasal Spray) now available on Express Scripts formularies starting Nov 22, 2024, aiding Type 1 Allergic Reactions' management.
news.summary: As of November 22, 2024, neffy has been added to the Express Scripts commercial formularies, enhancing accessibility for patients and caregivers dealing with Type 1 Allergic Reactions. This inclusion significantly broadens the reach of neffy, providing more individuals with access to this medication for managing the condition.

news.headline: AANA criticizes insurance provider for restricting anesthesia reimbursement, endangering CRNAs and patients.
news.summary: Rosemont, Illinois - On December 19, 2024, Medical Mutual became the most recent insurance provider to impose restrictions on anesthesia reimbursement. The American Association of Nurse Anesthesiology (AANA) reacted to this development by releasing a statement criticizing Medical Mutual's altered reimbursement policy. They expressed concerns regarding the impact this change would have, particularly referencing the potential consequences on patients and healthcare providers. This change comes amidst a larger trend of insurance providers scaling back reimbursements for anesthesia services. The exact figures regarding the reduction in reimbursement rates were not disclosed in the statement.

news.headline: Sendas Distribuidora S.A. intends to delist its American Depositary Shares from NYSE; decision made by the Board of Directors.
news.summary: On December 19, 2024, Sendas Distribuidora S.A. (B3: ASAI3; NYSE: ASAI) disclosed its plan to voluntarily delist its American Depositary Shares. The decision was made during a meeting of the Company's Board of Directors.

news.headline: Par Pacific announces 2025 capital expenditure guidance of $210M to $240M, aiming for strategic growth and infrastructure enhancement.
news.summary: Houston-based company Par Pacific Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: PARR) disclosed their projected capital expenditure and turnaround outlay guidance for 2025, falling between $210 million and $240 million. The announcement was made on December 19, 2024.

news.headline: Fitch assigns AA+ IDR to BC Hydro with stable outlook
Ecuador gains access to $500 million as IMF concludes EFF review
NEWSTAR buys 23 acres in Charlotte for new rental neighborhood with 159 homes called Long Farm. Expands in Charlotte.
news.summary: Today, NEWSTAR revealed the acquisition of 23 acres of land on Harrisburg Road in Charlotte, North Carolina. The company intends to develop a new single-family rental subdivision called Long Farm, boasting 159 homes. This venture marks an expansion for NEWSTAR in Charlotte.

news.headline: Amazon settled federal safety accusations by agreeing to a $145,000 penalty, with OSHA withdrawing citations at 9 of 10 warehouses.
news.summary: OSHA has decided to retract citations issued at nine out of 10 warehouses due to safety violations. The company has agreed to pay a penalty of $145,000 as part of the resolution.

news.headline: CACI Chairman, Michael Daniels, won 2024 NACD Directorship 100™ B. Kenneth West Lifetime Achievement Award in New York gala.
news.summary: In Reston, Virginia, CACI International Inc revealed that their Chairman of the Board, Michael (Mike) A. Daniels, received the esteemed 2024 National Association of Corporate Directors (NACD) Directorship 100™ B. Kenneth West Lifetime Achievement Award at the annual gala in New York. This award recognizes directors demonstrating outstanding service on public, private, and/or nonprofit boards. CACI International Inc is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol CACI.

news.headline: NewStar Exchange buys Hadley Crossing in Charlotte, NC and launches Delaware Statutory Trust offering. #RealEstate #Acquisition #Investment
news.summary: Today, ATLANTA - On December 19, 2024, NEWSTAR Exchange, a subdivision of NEWSTAR, revealed the acquisition of Hadley Crossing by Stella Homes, a single-family rental community in Charlotte, North Carolina. This includes the commencement of a new Delaware Statutory Trust for the acquisition.

news.headline: Sara Lee Bread and U.S. Hunger partnership reveals hidden realities of food insecurity. Americans unaware of impact.
news.summary: A recent report uncovered that many Americans are eager to combat food insecurity, despite a lack of awareness of its extensive effect on families nationwide. This revelation comes from a study conducted in HORSHAM, Pa., released on Dec. 19, 2024. The findings emphasize the critical need for assistance, indicating a gap in public knowledge regarding the scope of the issue. Sara Lee® Bread and U.S. have joined forces in a long-term collaboration to address this pressing concern.

news.headline: House to vote on GOP stopgap and debt-limit bill after 6pm ET
Kroger contracts $5.0B accelerated share repurchase with Wells Fargo and Citibank.
news.summary: In Cincinnati, Ohio, on December 19, 2024, The Kroger Co. (NYSE: KR) revealed their agreement for an Accelerated Share Repurchase ("ASR") with Wells Fargo Bank and Citibank, N.A. The company plans to repurchase a total of $5.0 billion in stock through this arrangement.

news.headline: Chimera Investment Corp declared Q4 2024 dividend of $0.37/share, payable on Jan 31, 2025 to shareholders as of Dec 31, 2024.
news.summary: In New York, the Board of Directors at Chimera Investment Corporation has declared a fourth quarter cash dividend of $0.37 for each common share. This dividend will be paid on January 31, 2025, to common stockholders listed on records as of December 31, 2024. The ex-dividend date is also on December 31, 2024. Chimera Investment Corporation is a REIT focused on real estate investments and is publicly traded.

news.headline: FCPT buys MercyOne clinic property for $2.8M in Iowa, under triple net lease with 5 years left.
news.summary: Based in Mill Valley, California, Four Corners Property Trust, a real estate investment trust listed on NYSE as FCPT, focuses on owning and acquiring high-quality, net-leased restaurant and retail properties. The company recently acquired a MercyOne outpatient clinic property in Iowa for $2.8 million. Situated in a robust retail area, the property operates under a triple net lease with around five years left on the agreement. This acquisition marks another strategic move by FCPT to enhance its investment portfolio.

news.headline: Xali Gold grants 4,100,000 stock options at $0.05/share. Annual General Meeting voting results announced.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on December 19, 2024, Xali Gold Corp. (TSXV:XGC) announced the granting of 4,100,000 stock options under its Omnibus Equity Incentive Plan. These options are offered to specific directors, officers, and consultants of the Company, allowing them to purchase common shares at a price of $0.05 per share. The options can be exercised within a five-year period from the date of granting.

news.headline: Preferred Bank discovered an error in its calculation, leading to $8.1 million pre-tax expense in Q4, reducing EPS by $0.43.
news.summary: On December 19, 2024, Preferred Bank (NASDAQ: PFBC) disclosed that it identified an unexplained variance in its evaluation of the right of use asset (ROU) and lease liabilities. The independent commercial bank stated that due to this discrepancy, it will report $8.1 million in pre-tax occupancy expense for the fourth quarter of 2024. Consequently, the error will result in a reduction of approximately $0.43 in diluted earnings per share for the same quarter on an after-tax basis.

news.headline: Mission Produce Inc. (NASDAQ:AVO) Q4 earnings: $0.28 per share, revenue: $354.4 million, exceeding analyst estimate of $220.4 million.
news.summary: After Thursday's closing bell, Mission Produce Inc. (NASDAQ:AVO) released its fourth-quarter results. The quarter showed a beat in expectations with earnings of 28 cents per share and revenue of $354.4 million, outperforming the analyst consensus of $220.4 million. The company completed an outstanding fiscal year 2024, generating $1.23 billion in revenue and $107.8 million in profits.

news.headline: FlexIt introduces FlexTogether™ for global connection in fitness, featuring AI Class Creator™. Partnered with Samsung and LG Electronics.
news.summary: A company based in New York, FlexIt, has recently introduced FlexTogether™, a platform aimed at promoting global interaction and connectivity through unique wellness activities. Key to this launch is the AI Class Creator™ Technology, a new proprietary feature that elevates personalized workout sessions and social engagements. This initiative is strengthened by collaborations with major technology companies such as Samsung and LG Electronics. FlexIt continues its commitment to enhancing wellness experiences, with this recent launch serving as a significant milestone in their ongoing efforts.

news.headline: Sculptor closes CLO XXXIV worth $408 million, part of issuing/refinancing 13 CLOs this year.
news.summary: Jimmy Levin, the CIO and Executive Managing Partner of Sculptor Capital Management, announced the closure of an exceptional year for their ICS business. They successfully issued or refinanced a total of 13 CLOs. This achievement marks a significant milestone for the Firm in their efforts to strengthen their position in the market. The news was made public on December 19, 2024, from their headquarters in New York.

news.headline: William Merz has been appointed as the new Senior VP of Aerospace and Defense Technologies at Oceaneering, succeeding Philip Beierl.
news.summary: In Houston, William Merz has been appointed as the new Senior Vice President of Aerospace and Defense Technologies for Oceaneering. He will succeed Philip Beierl in this role. The transition marks a strategic move for the company, as Merz brings his wealth of experience to the position. Oceaneering is a key player in the industry, specializing in innovative technologies for aerospace and defense sectors. The change in leadership is expected to further enhance the company's offerings in these areas. This development showcases Oceaneering's commitment to growth and excellence in its sector.

news.headline: Kingkey Financial International: Potential Collaboration on Artificial Intelligence
Kingkey Financial International is considering a potential strategic collaboration with Whalefin.

Reitmans (Canada) Limited reports Q3 results: $187.7M in revenue, 2.9% decrease; net earnings drop to $2.1M. Reducing store count due to warmer weather impact. EBITDA decreased to $3.8M; Cash at $123.1M. CEO focuses on inventory management and strategic growth.
news.summary: Reitmans (Canada) Limited reported its financial results for the fiscal 2025 third quarter, with net revenues decreasing by 2.9% to $187.7 million primarily due to store closures and weather conditions. Gross margin increased to 57.3%, but adjusted EBITDA decreased to $3.8 million. Net earnings also decreased to $2.1 million. The company's strategic focus on inventory control and less promotion helped achieve these results. The company opened three new stores during the quarter and is looking to expand further. In terms of financials, net revenues for the year-to-date fiscal 2025 were $568.9 million, while gross profit was $328.8 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $28.0 million. Cash reserves stood at $123.1 million as of November 2, 2024. CEO Andrea Limbardi cited menswear success at RW&CO and positive customer responses to the fall collection as highlights of the quarter. The company remains focused on adapting to evolving customer needs and modernizing its distribution center.

news.headline: SIR Corp. reports Q1 2025 results: food revenue $58.7M, SSS decreased by 6.6%, closed Jack Astor's & facing cyber incident.
news.summary: SIR Royalty Income Fund announced that SIR Corp has filed financial results for Q1 2025. Food and beverage revenue from corporate restaurant operations decreased by 1.1% to $58.7 million, with consolidated Same Store Sales declining by 6.6%. A cybersecurity incident on September 26, 2024, led to revenue decline and increased costs until October 23. SIR closed a restaurant and entered a Twelfth Amending Agreement with its Senior Lender on December 6, 2024, adjusting financial covenants and interest rates. Revenue decline attributed to various factors but offset slightly by new restaurant openings. Jack Astor's had a 9.1% decline in Same Store Sales. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Scholastic (NASDAQ:SCHL) Q2 results disappoint: Earnings at $1.82 per share, revenue at $544.6 million, missing estimates.
news.summary: After the closing bell on Thursday, Scholastic Corp. (NASDAQ:SCHL) released its second-quarter financial results. The company reported earnings of $1.82 per share, falling short of the analyst consensus of $2.30. Quarterly revenue was $544.6 million, missing the analyst consensus of $553.97 million and declining from $562.6 million in the previous year. Revenues in the Children’s Book Publishing and Distribution segment also experienced a decrease. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Mattr Corp. closes debt offering with $129.3 million gross proceeds. Subscription receipts to convert into Notes.
news.summary: Toronto, on December 19, 2024, Mattr Corp. ("Mattr" or the "Company") (TSX: MATR) has officially completed its private offering of debt subscription receipts (Subscription Receipts) raising about $129.3 million in total gross proceeds. The funds from the Offering, after deducting the underwriters' fee and expenses, are currently held in escrow. They will remain there until specific conditions are met or waived. Afterward, the Subscription Receipts will convert into Notes.

news.headline: Novanta to present at CJS Securities 25th Annual New Ideas for the New Year Investor Conference on January 14, 2025.
news.summary: Novanta Inc., a technology partner for medical and advanced technology equipment manufacturers, shared that its management will attend the CJS Securities 25th Annual New Ideas for the New Year Investor Conference on January 14, 2025. The event, which will be held virtually, will showcase Novanta's core technology solutions. Novanta, under the ticker NOVT on Nasdaq, is a prominent company in this sector. This participation at the conference highlights the company's commitment to staying abreast of new opportunities and innovations in the industry.

news.headline: ALLETE (NYSE: ALE) gets FERC approval for deal with CPP Investments and GIP, bringing them closer to transaction benefits.
news.summary: In Duluth, Minnesota, ALLETE, Inc. (NYSE: ALE) received approval from the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) for its planned transaction involving Canada Pension Plan Investment Board (CPP Investments) and Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP). The approval signifies a significant step forward for ALLETE, bringing them closer to the benefits of the deal for stakeholders. The collaboration entails key numbers in terms of investments and infrastructure development, enhancing opportunities for customers, communities, and employees.

news.headline: Standard Motor Products released 2,367 new part numbers in 2024, including from Vehicle Control and Temperature Control divisions.
news.summary: In a recent announcement made by Standard Motor Products, Inc. (SMP) in New York on December 19, 2024, the company revealed the introduction of a remarkable 2,367 new part numbers for the year. These additions include offerings from SMP's Vehicle Control and Temperature Control divisions. The newly released parts provide expanded coverage for late-model import vehicles and demonstrate SMP's commitment to enhancing its product range.

news.headline: AAR Corp. resolves FCPA investigations with DOJ and SEC in Dec 2024 after potential violations.
news.summary: In Wood Dale, Illinois, on December 19, 2024, AAR CORP. (NYSE: AIR) revealed that they have settled with the Department of Justice ("DOJ") and the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC") regarding potential violations. The resolutions mark a significant development for the Company.

news.headline: Nippon Steel declines to commit to maintaining production at plants
Timbercreek Financial announces $0.0575 per share monthly dividend, payable on Jan 15, 2025 to holders on record by Dec 31, 2024.
news.summary: On December 19, 2024, Timbercreek Financial (TSX: TF) declared a monthly cash dividend of $0.0575 per common share to be paid out on January 15, 2025. The dividend is for shareholders of record as of December 31, 2024.

news.headline: VR Resources secures $400k through private placement for drilling projects at Silverback and Empire in 2025.
news.summary: The company has confirmed a flow-through financing arrangement of $400,000. The funds will be allocated promptly towards drilling activities scheduled for January.

news.headline: Northview Residential REIT reveals December payout for investors.
news.summary: A recent report indicates that the unemployment rate in the US fell to 3.8% in April, with nonfarm payrolls increasing by 263,000. This surpassed market expectations of 180,000 jobs added. The labor force participation rate remained steady at 63.2%. The report also revealed that average hourly earnings rose by 0.2% in April and have increased by 3.2% over the year. The positive job market data suggests a strong and stable economy in the US.

news.headline: Xcel Brands (NASDAQ: XELB) to release Q3 2024 results on Dec 20; Earnings call on Dec 23 at 5:00 p.m. ET.
news.summary: On December 19, 2024, Xcel Brands, Inc. disclosed its plans to release the financial results for the third quarter of 2024 on December 20, 2024. The company, trading as NASDAQ: XELB, will conduct a conference call with investors on December 23, 2024, at 5:00 p.m. ET.

news.headline: Embecta Corp. to present at J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference on Jan 15, 2025, in San Francisco at 7:30 am PT / 10:30 am ET. Nasdaq: EMBC.
news.summary: Embecta Corp. disclosed that they will participate in the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference in San Francisco on January 15, 2025. The presentation is scheduled for 7:30 a.m. PT / 10:30 a.m. ET. The company's stock is listed as EMBC on Nasdaq.

news.headline: AutoZone promotes Bailey Childress to Senior VP, Omnichannel & Merchandising Support, and Luke Rauch to Senior VP, Merchandising & Global Sourcing. 218 characters.
news.summary: In Memphis, Tennessee on December 19, 2024, AutoZone, Inc. declared promotions for two Senior Vice Presidents. Bailey Childress, previously serving as Vice President of Merchandising, has been elevated to Senior Vice President, overseeing Omnichannel and Merchandising Support. Luke Rauch, formerly the Vice President of Merchandising, has been advanced to Senior Vice President, managing Merchandising and Global Sourcing. These promotions affect the individuals in notable positions at AutoZone, Inc. (NYSE: AZO).

news.headline: Wallbox (NYSE: WBX) notified by NYSE for non-compliance with share price listing rule; average closing price below threshold.
news.summary: In Barcelona, Spain, Wallbox N.V. disclosed that it was informed by the New York Stock Exchange on November 21, 2024, regarding its non-compliance with Section 802.01C of the NYSE Listed Company Manual. The notification was issued due to the average closing price of Wallbox's Class A ordinary shares being below a certain threshold. Wallbox N.V. (NYSE: WBX) is a prominent global provider of electric vehicle charging and energy management solutions.

news.headline: Mission Produce (NASDAQ:AVO) Q4 earnings exceeded estimates by 250%, reporting EPS of $0.28. More details on Benzinga.com.
news.summary: On December 19, 2024, Mission Produce (NASDAQ: AVO) disclosed its Q4 earnings after market close. The company surpassed estimated earnings significantly, with an EPS of $0.28, exceeding expectations by 250.0%. This positive financial result provides essential insights for investors. For more detailed information, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Scholastic (NASDAQ:SCHL) Q2 earnings report showed a -21.0% miss, reporting EPS $1.82 vs estimate.
news.summary: Educational publisher Scholastic (NASDAQ:SCHL) released its Q2 earnings report on December 19, 2024, at 04:01 PM. The company fell short of expected earnings by -21.0%, with an EPS of $1.82 compared to the estimated figure of [provide missing number]).For further details on Scholastic's Q2 earnings report, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: FedEx (NYSE:FDX) stock rose on 2nd quarter EPS of $4.05 (beat $3.91 est) and $22B revenue (missed $22.11B est). FedEx Freight revenue and profit lower.
news.summary: FedEx Corp. (NYSE:FDX) released its second-quarter earnings report post Thursday's market close. The key numbers include adjusted earnings of $4.05 per share, surpassing the anticipated $3.91. Quarterly revenue stood at $22 billion, slightly below the projected $22.11 billion. The company noted that reduced FedEx Freight revenue and profit had a negative impact on consolidated operating results. This decline was attributed to ongoing weakness in U.S. industrial production, impacting less-than-truckload industry demand. However, the unfavorable FedEx Freight outcomes were mitigated by cost savings from the Federal Express DRIVE program and improved base yields across all transportation segments. FedEx Freight segment operating results saw a decrease during the quarter due to... (The complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com)

news.headline: Nike Q2 earnings: Revenue beat at $12.35B, EPS beat at 78 cents, shares jump. Company recovers with strong results.
news.summary: In the latest financial report, Nike Inc (NYSE:NKE) disclosed its Q2 earnings for fiscal 2025 after markets closed on Thursday. The athletic footwear and apparel giant posted second-quarter revenue of $12.35 billion, surpassing analyst predictions of $12.13 billion. Earnings for the quarter stood at 78 cents per share, outperforming estimates of 65 cents per share, marking a positive outcome for the company.Overall, total revenues witnessed an 8% decrease compared to the previous year. Sales in North America, Greater China, Europe, Asia Pacific, and Latin America were down approximately 8%, 8%, 7%, and 3% respectively in the second quarter. Nike Direct revenues plummeted by 13% year-over-year, totaling $5 billion, while Nike Brand revenues saw a 7% drop, amounting to $12 billion. The Wholesale sector also experienced a decline.For further details, the complete news article can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Dow Jones falls by over 260 points on retail sales, with Nvidia dropping as Tesla hits a record high.
news.summary: The Dow Jones and other stock market indexes experienced a decline today following a retail sales report. Nvidia saw a significant decrease, while Tesla reached another all-time high. The Dow Jones dropped by over 260 points. The market is closely monitoring the upcoming Federal Reserve decision. Tesla's stock price surged on what analysts describe as a 'bull case.'

news.headline: Roadzen Inc. closes public offering, sells 2,300,000 shares at $1.25/share, raising $2,875,000. All shares sold by the Company.
news.summary: In New York on December 17, 2024, Roadzen Inc. (Nasdaq: RDZN), a top player in AI in insurance and mobility, revealed the completion of its public offering of 2,300,000 ordinary shares (or Pre-funded Warrants in place of shares), which included the sale of 300,000 shares through the underwriter's option for additional shares. The offering was priced at $1.25 per share, resulting in total gross proceeds of $2,875,000, excluding underwriting discounts, commissions, and Offering expenses. The Company sold all the ordinary shares (or Pre-funded Warrants) in the offering.

news.headline: Investor suit against Hawk Token creators alleges it acts as unregistered security due to legal characteristics.
news.summary: Litigants have alleged that the HAWK Token possesses all the attributes of an unregistered security per known legal standards.
